name: human-in-the-loop
description: Enable AI assistants like GitHub Copilot to interact with humans through intuitive VS Code dialogs. Provides tools for user input, choices, confirmations, and feedback.

languageModelTools.name: human_getUserInput
languageModelTools.userDescription: Ask the user to type in text, a number, or some value in an input box.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: IMPORTANT: This is the PRIMARY tool for asking the human user any question. Call this tool to ask the user a question and collect their short typed response. Opens a simple input dialog. CRITICAL: Do NOT put your plans, explanations, or long content in the prompt parameter. Instead, present your detailed content (plans, summaries, code, etc.) directly in the chat response first — Copilot Chat renders Markdown natively. Then call this tool with ONLY a short, specific question as the prompt. Example: present your plan in the chat, then call this tool with prompt='Do you approve this plan? Any changes?' Parameters: title (string, required) - Short title for the input box; prompt (string, required) - A brief, specific question (1-2 sentences max); defaultValue (string, optional) - Pre-filled default; inputType (string, optional) - 'text', 'integer', or 'float'.

prompt.description: The prompt/question to show to the user

defaultValue.description: Default value to pre-fill in the input field

languageModelTools.name: human_getUserChoice
languageModelTools.userDescription: Present a list of options for the user to pick from.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: IMPORTANT: Call this tool to present a list of options to the human user and let them select one or multiple choices. Opens a selection dialog. CRITICAL: Do NOT put explanations or long descriptions in the prompt. Instead, explain the options and context in the chat response first — Copilot Chat renders Markdown natively. Then call this tool with ONLY a brief prompt and the list of choices. Parameters: title (string, required) - Short title; prompt (string, required) - Brief question (1-2 sentences); choices (string array, required) - List of options; allowMultiple (boolean, optional) - Allow multiple selections (default: false).

prompt.description: The prompt/question to show to the user

allowMultiple.description: Whether user can select multiple choices

languageModelTools.name: human_getMultilineInput
languageModelTools.userDescription: Open a multi-line text editor for the user to write longer content.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Call this tool ONLY when the user needs to write or paste LARGE amounts of text — multiple paragraphs, entire code files, long specifications, or content that requires a full text editor. Do NOT use for answering questions or giving short feedback — use human_getUserInput instead. CRITICAL: Do NOT put your plans or content in the prompt. Present context in the chat first, then call with a brief prompt. Parameters: title (string, required) - Short title; prompt (string, required) - Brief instruction (1-2 sentences); defaultValue (string, optional) - Pre-filled text.

title.description: Title of the multiline input dialog

languageModelTools.name: human_showConfirmation
languageModelTools.userDescription: Ask the user for yes/no confirmation before performing an action.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: IMPORTANT: Call this tool to ask the human user for explicit yes/no confirmation before proceeding. Shows a dialog with Yes and No buttons. CRITICAL: Do NOT put long explanations in the message. Present the full context in the chat first, then call this tool with ONLY a brief confirmation question. Parameters: title (string, required) - Short title; message (string, required) - Brief confirmation question (1-2 sentences).

languageModelTools.name: human_showInfoMessage
languageModelTools.userDescription: Display a notification message to the user.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Call this tool to display a brief notification to the human user that requires acknowledgement. Shows a dialog with an OK button. CRITICAL: Only use for short notifications (task completed, status update). Do NOT use to present plans, explanations, or long content — present those directly in the chat response instead. Parameters: title (string, required) - Short title; message (string, required) - Brief notification text (1-3 sentences).

languageModelTools.name: human_healthCheck
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check if the Human-in-the-Loop extension is running.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Checks if the Human-in-the-Loop extension is active and operational. Returns system information including platform, extension version, and a list of all available tools. Call this to verify the extension is working before using other human interaction tools.

chatParticipants.name: human
chatParticipants.description: Interact with users through dialogs - get input, choices, confirmations, and display messages.

commands.name: input
commands.description: Ask the user for text, number, or other single-line input

commands.name: choice
commands.description: Present choices for the user to select from

commands.name: multiline
commands.description: Ask the user for multi-line text input

commands.name: confirm
commands.description: Ask the user for yes/no confirmation

commands.name: info
commands.description: Display an informational message to the user

commands.name: health
commands.description: Check extension health and platform info
